title: Iran transportation infrastructure history — railway network length/construction timeline (incl. Trans-Iranian Railway 1927-1938), road network length by era, Bandar Abbas port throughput
source_org: Compiled from Wikipedia (Trans-Iranian Railway; Rail transport in Iran), Parstimes.com (Brief History of Ministry of Road & Transportation, itself citing Iranian Ministry of Road records), and port-industry trade press (Lloyd's List Top 100 Ports 2019, Container News)
frequency: irregular milestone snapshots, not a continuous annual series
units: described per-row in CSV (km, tons, TEU, rials, dates, percent)
license_terms: Wikipedia content CC BY-SA; Parstimes.com and trade press content used for factual citation only, not reproduced verbatim beyond short attributed figures
notes: Fills the transportation-infrastructure gap explicitly flagged in the user's candidate-direction list: 'rail km built (Trans-Iranian Railway construction timeline), paved road km, port throughput'. The Pahlavi-era-primary-extraction archive already has RAILWAY INCOME/EXPENSE and FREIGHT/PASSENGER TRAFFIC tables for 1953/54-1960/61 (see data/raw/pahlavi-era-primary-extraction/) -- this dataset deliberately does NOT duplicate that operational/financial data and instead focuses on NETWORK LENGTH and construction/institutional history, which is a genuinely different angle. Several figures in this dataset are recorded as EXPLICIT RANGES/contested-pairs per bookkeeping.md's contested-estimate policy rather than silently picking one number: (1) Trans-Iranian Railway total length 1,392km vs 1,394km (likely rounding/routing-measurement differences, not a real dispute); (2) 1938 tunnel count 224 (original Trans-Iranian line) vs 105 (modern 2000s full national network -- different scope, not contradictory); (3) end-of-Pahlavi-era road network length: 26,000km 'main and secondary roads only' (Parstimes, narrower category) vs ~50,000km broader estimate from general secondary sources (likely includes rural/access roads) -- flagged as a genuine sourcing ambiguity needing a primary Iranian government statistical yearbook to resolve definitively, not resolved here. Khorramshahr port historical throughput was searched for specifically (per the user's brief) but NOT FOUND in a form with real historical tonnage figures -- only a recent unattributed '62% year-on-year growth' percentage was located, not usable as a data point; flagged as a dead end for this pass, worth a dedicated future search of Iran Ports & Maritime Organization's own historical statistical yearbooks (data/raw/iran-ports-maritime-org/ already exists in this project with 2024 tariff booklets, but no historical throughput series). Airport passenger traffic was intentionally NOT re-hunted here because data/raw/iran-aviation-history/encyclopedia-com-iranair-company-history/ already has a solid IranAir (airline-level) passengers/employees/revenue series 1950-2003 -- a dedicated Mehrabad-Airport-specific (as opposed to airline-specific) passenger count was searched for but not found as a distinct historical series, likely because pre-1980s Iranian airport-level (as opposed to airline-level) statistics were not commonly published or are not indexed online.

failures.result: no historical (pre-2020s) tonnage/TEU series found; only a recent unattributed percentage-growth figure located, not recorded as a data point per no-fabrication rule

failures.url: Mehrabad Airport (Tehran) historical passenger traffic, airport-level (distinct from IranAir airline-level data already in the database)
failures.result: not found as a distinct pre-1980s series; genuine gap
